behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watching semantics (recursive watch coverage across directories). A unit/integration fake can simulate events but can hide real watcher behavior differences. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both push to System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp input root and real output dir. Create a nested subdirectory, write a file inside it, and assert exactly one transformed output appears. This requires real OS file events and actual watcher recursion behavior.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly deduplication logic on path + time window; minimum failure boundary is local decision logic. No required OS/network/subprocess, no model output dependency, no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real filesystem events; in-process calls with a fake clock can expose the real failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call dedup component directly with the same canonical file path twice and controlled timestamps (or fake clock), asserting first request accepted and second rejected within window, then accepted after window expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS event emission patterns. In-process mocks usually emit idealized events and can miss duplicate-event bursts/coalescing quirks. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE require System. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML content and no multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real watched dir, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., temp-write + rename or repeated append/close), and assert one processing/output record for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at OS watcher/inode boundary during live directory deletion/recreation. Mocks hide re-subscription and handle invalidation behavior. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE require System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text excludes model-dependent decisions and multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon, remove watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering correctness here depends on concurrent arrival timing plus filesystem timestamp metadata/resolution under real writes. Integration fakes can enforce artificial order and miss race/timestamp granularity failures. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE indicate System. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML decision quality and no multi-agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times (or staggered writes within timestamp resolution), then assert processing order matches creation timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real write-in-progress timing and filesystem event timing. In-process mocks typically model complete writes and can hide partial-read races. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE require System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML output dependence or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Open a file in the watched dir, write partial content, keep handle open, trigger/observe arrival event, and verify daemon delays processing until writer closes and final content is stable; then assert processed output uses complete content.
